examples.title: Process Assessment Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm for conducting a basic process assessment
examples.code:
PROCEDURE ProcessAssessment(processList, assessmentModel)
BEGIN
 results ← empty map
 FOR EACH process IN processList DO
 scores ← empty list
 FOR EACH attribute IN assessmentModel.attributes DO
 rating ← RateProcessAttribute(process, attribute)
 scores.append(rating)
 END FOR
 capabilityLevel ← CalculateCapabilityLevel(scores)
 results[process] ← capabilityLevel
 END FOR
 GenerateAssessmentReport(results)
 RETURN results
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the basic structure of a process assessment algorithm that evaluates individual processes against defined attributes and calculates capability levels.

examples.title: CMM Maturity Level Determination
examples.description: Algorithm to determine organizational maturity level based on KPA satisfaction
examples.code:
PROCEDURE DetermineMaturityLevel(kpaStatus)
BEGIN
 maturityLevels ← ["Initial", "Repeatable", "Defined", "QuantitativelyManaged", "Optimizing"]
 
 FOR level FROM 5 DOWNTO 1 DO
 requiredKPAs ← GetRequiredKPAs(level)
 satisfiedKPAs ← 0
 
 FOR EACH kpa IN requiredKPAs DO
 IF kpaStatus[kpa] == "satisfied" THEN
 satisfiedKPAs ← satisfiedKPAs + 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 IF satisfiedKPAs == requiredKPAs.length THEN
 RETURN maturityLevels[level-1]
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN "Below Level 1"
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This algorithm checks satisfaction of Key Process Areas to determine the appropriate maturity level, starting from Level 5 and working downwards.

examples.title: Process Improvement Cycle (PDCA Implementation)
examples.code:
PROCEDURE PDCA_ImprovementCycle(processArea)
BEGIN
 WHILE improvementNeeded DO
 // PLAN Phase
 issues ← AssessProcess(processArea)
 improvementPlan ← IdentifyImprovements(issues)
 
 // DO Phase
 ImplementImprovements(improvementPlan)
 
 // CHECK Phase
 metricsBefore ← CollectMetrics(processArea)
 metricsAfter ← CollectMetrics(processArea)
 comparison ← CompareMetrics(metricsBefore, metricsAfter)
 
 // ACT Phase
 IF comparison.success THEN
 StandardizeImprovements(processArea)
 ELSE
 ReviseImprovementPlan(improvementPlan)
 END IF
 
 LogResults(processArea, comparison)
 END WHILE
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This pseudocode implements the Plan-Do-Check-Act cycle for continuous process improvement, showing how each phase connects to form an iterative improvement loop.
examples.timeComplexity: O(i) where i=number of improvement iterations

examples.title: Defect Density Calculation
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate defect density for quality measurement
examples.code:
PROCEDURE CalculateDefectDensity(totalDefects, linesOfCode)
BEGIN
 IF linesOfCode == 0 THEN
 RETURN 0
 END IF
 
 KLOC ← linesOfCode / 1000
 defectDensity ← totalDefects / KLOC
 
 RETURN defectDensity
END PROCEDURE

PROCEDURE InterpretDefectDensity(density)
BEGIN
 IF density < 1.0 THEN
 RETURN "Very Good - World Class"
 ELSE IF density < 2.0 THEN
 RETURN "Good"
 ELSE IF density < 5.0 THEN
 RETURN "Average"
 ELSE IF density < 10.0 THEN
 RETURN "Poor"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Very Poor"
 END IF
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: These procedures calculate defect density per thousand lines of code (KLOC) and provide interpretation guidelines for assessing software quality.
